check_api_or_status_claims.goal: Prevent stale public claims in generated content.
steps: Check docs/CANONICAL_API.md for names and syntax.
Check docs/spec/COMPILER_REFERENCE.md for compiler status and phase labels.

create_examples_or_tutorials.goal: Create runnable examples that match current APIs and implementation status.
steps: Start from examples/getting_started or examples/compiler for style and structure.
Check docs/CANONICAL_API.md before introducing public API calls.
Prefer CPU-promised paths unless the example is explicitly hardware-marked.
Add or update sample tests when the example becomes part of active documentation.

plan_code_changes.goal: Use the map to locate specs, source files, and tests before editing code.
steps: Identify the entity for the target subsystem.
Read its normative references before implementation.
Inspect implemented_in and tested_by targets before editing.
Update docs or examples when public behavior changes.

write_or_update_docs.goal: Keep documentation aligned with canonical authorities.
steps: Read docs/README.md to identify the normative authority for the topic.
Use this knowledge map to find related guides, tests, examples, and implementation surfaces.
Use canonical API names from docs/CANONICAL_API.md and docs/spec/PYTHON_API_SPEC.md.
Avoid archive docs as authority; cite them only as historical background.
Run docs lint and any targeted tests for changed claims.

api.description: A public Python, CLI, runtime, or ABI surface.

backend.description: A hardware or runtime target backend.

concept.description: A project concept such as tiling, effects, conformance, or diagnostics.

dialect.description: An MLIR dialect or operation family.

doc.description: A documentation authority, guide, tutorial, or reference page.

ir_layer.description: One layer in the Tessera compiler IR stack.

pass.description: A compiler pass or named lowering pipeline.

runtime_component.description: A runtime subsystem, ABI component, profiler, or execution adapter.

test_suite.description: A test family, validation suite, or benchmark gate.

defined_by.description: The entity's authoritative definition is in the target doc or entity.

depends_on.description: The entity needs the target concept or component to be understood or changed safely.

explained_by.description: The entity is explained by the target guide, tutorial, or README.

implemented_in.description: The entity is implemented or scaffolded in the target source path or component.

lowers_to.description: The source IR layer or construct lowers into the target IR layer or construct.

phase_status.description: The entity's implementation/planning status is described by the target status entity or doc.

supersedes.description: The source entity replaces or overrides the target historical entity.

tested_by.description: The entity is covered by the target test suite.
